expectations.text: states-situation-plainly: The draft describes the situation in factual, neutral terms without emotional language or blame
expectations.evidence: 'We have a blocker on the checkout flow integration that's been sitting for 10 days now... I've pinged Sasha (their lead) three times on Slack and sent an email last Thursday — nothing back.' Neutral, factual.

expectations.text: includes-impact: The draft mentions the concrete impact (sprint deadline, integration risk, or standup accountability)
expectations.evidence: 'Sprint ends Friday. Without the spec, we won't finish the integration on time.' Clear deadline impact.

expectations.text: references-prior-attempts: The draft mentions that attempts were already made to resolve directly
expectations.evidence: 'I've pinged Sasha (their lead) three times on Slack and sent an email last Thursday — nothing back.' Specific and documented.

expectations.text: clear-ask: The draft includes a specific ask — either from Keiko to intervene or a concrete next step
expectations.evidence: Offers 3 numbered options: Keiko reaches out to Sasha's manager, call with Payments, or scoping a stub/deferral. Multiple actionable paths provided.

expectations.text: no-blamey-language: The draft avoids language that attacks Sasha or the Payments team
expectations.evidence: 'I'm not sure if there's something going on on their side I'm not aware of' — explicitly considers good-faith explanations. No blame language.
